markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define user/model-facing diagnostics when a bundled dependency or parser fails to initialize. This MT belongs to NativeDependencyAndPackaging. Make WP-009 native-by-default and fully packaged inside Handshake.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
No proof may require manually launched external services or tools except explicitly allowed model/GGUF/tensor/CUI-portable surfaces.
Bundled frontend/editor libraries must be lockfile-governed, license-reviewed, and callable through Handshake UI/runtime surfaces.
scope.expected_behavior: DependencyFailureMessaging leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: DependencyFailureMessaging is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: Implemented in wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 commit f69c5bbf; proof commands green per delegate report (vitest/cargo/playwright; MT-032 aggregate check:dependency-policy 8/8 pass, app suite 313/313). Pending adversarial code review before READY_FOR_VALIDATION.

concerns: Real DependencyFailureBanner (data-testid=dependency-failure-banner, role=alert) wired at the REAL DocumentView mount site next to TiptapEditor (git show f69c5bbf confirms the JSX insertion) and the harness. TiptapEditor's buildGuardedDocumentExtensions returns null on failure and renders nothing while the SIBLING banner explains it -> no blank screen.

vitest uses mocks ONLY as failure injectors (operator rule): the setMonaco/Tiptap factory seams take the EXACT production reporting path (constructWorker/buildWp009ExtensionSet -> dependencyFailures.report). Banner shows dependency+component, degraded-mode + restart/reinstall guidance, dismiss + re-surface on new failure.

SCOPE NOTE (not a defect): Monaco is not mounted in any shipping product view, so a real-app Monaco worker failure cannot occur yet; the Monaco failure path is proven in the harness/vitest. The only real-app editor mount (DocumentView/TiptapEditor) is banner-covered. No uncovered blank-screen editor path found.
evasion_probes: Traced: a Monaco worker construction throw -> constructWorker catch -> dependencyFailures.report -> banner subscription re-renders (proven in test). A Tiptap extension throw -> guarded factory -> report -> banner. A document-extension throw -> TiptapEditor returns null -> banner sibling visible (container has no .tiptap-editor but shows 'What you can do').
Banner catches failures reported before mount via useState(()=>registry.list())+effect catch-up.
evidence_checked: git show f69c5bbf DocumentView.tsx + TiptapEditor.tsx diffs.

vitest 56/56 green across app/src/lib/dependency_policy/*, DependencyFailureBanner.test.tsx, worker_map.test.ts, extension_set.test.ts. DependencyFailureBanner.test.tsx 5 cases (incl render-nothing-no-failure, dismiss/re-surface).
grep: only one <TiptapEditor mount site, banner-adjacent.

evidence: Implementation commit f69c5bbf on feat/WP-KERNEL-009; adversarial code review verdict PASS (refutation attempts failed); runtime evidence: check:dependency-policy 8/8 exit 0 (real builds), cargo --lib dependency_policy:: 31/31, vitest 67/67 post-hardening.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
